title: pyrus pekinensis
intro: Pyrus pekinensis, also known as the Chinese Pear or Asian Pear, is a species of deciduous tree or shrub native to China, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Pyrus pekinensis include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. The herbal preparations of Pyrus pekinensis often include the fruit, leaves, and bark, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions for medicinal purposes.
